name: Currant Scones
recipeIngredient: 6 tbsp (3/4 stick) chilled unsalted butter, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 425°F.
2. Lightly flour a large baking sheet.
3. In a large bowl, mix together the flour, sugar, baking soda, and salt.
4. Add the butter and rub it in with your fingertips until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
5. Mix in the dried currants.
6. Mix in the beaten egg and enough buttermilk to form a soft dough.
7. Turn the dough out onto a floured surface. Pat it into a 3/4-inch thick round.
8. Using a 2-1/2 inch round cookie cutter, cut out rounds of scones. Gather the scraps, press them together, and pat again into a 3/4-inch thick round.
9. Cut out additional rounds.
10. Transfer the scones to the prepared baking sheet.
11. Brush the tops with milk.
12. Bake until the scones are golden brown and cooked through, about 18 minutes.
13. Serve warm with butter or whipped cream and jam.
